Meaning: Multi-dimensional arrays with longitude, latitude, depth, and time
A user or system that accesses, downloads, or uses the free and open marine data provided by the service
A specific temporal resolution or subset in a product
Original storage format used by the producer before it is converted into something else
A dataset with a unique ID

Details: Confusingly, Copernicus Marine also uses this acronym for the Scientific And Technical Advisory Committee. In the current context the we always mean SpatioTemporal Asset Catalog. It is a convention to describe geotemporal data.

Meaning: Physical quantities stored in the array

Details: The Web Map Tile Service (WMTS) is an international standard developed by the Open Geospatial Consortium (OGC) for serving digital map data using a cache of pre-rendered image tiles. Within the context of the package, WMTS is primarily used for rapid data visualization and inspection rather than for direct numerical analysis.

Meaning: Open-source specification and data format used to store large, multidimensional arrays and associated metadata
Details: Unlike traditional monolithic files, Zarr is built specifically for object storage (like [Amazon S3](https://aws.amazon.com/s3/)). It stores data in a directory-like structure where metadata and data chunks are separate, allowing for high-speed remote access without downloading entire files.
